title: Schiedeella nonantzin
taxonomy: Schiedeella nonantzin is a species of orchid native to Mexico. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Schiedeella is part of the tribe Cranichideae and subfamily Orchidoideae. This orchid is named after the goddess Coatlicue, a deity from Aztec mythology. Schiedeella nonantzin is further classified as a species of the subgenus Schiedeellinae.
uses: Schiedeella nonantzin is a rare orchid species that has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Research suggests that extracts from this plant may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could be useful in treating various health conditions. The plant's root is said to have been used by indigenous communities to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments.
